body:
TCA wake d3e1191e -- STATUS (Step 2 blocked, escalating rather than looping further).

STEP 1 COMPILE GATE: PASS. Note: SLS(20)'s own manifest (arrived 21min after the wake instruction, same chain d3e1191e) explicitly superseded SLS(19), so I compiled SLS(20) instead -- archived SLS19 + both SLS18 manifests. Compile: 1 warning first pass (compiler false-positive on a fully-loop-initialized sesMap[24] array in the new session-map sync fix) -- added ArrayInitialize as a no-op-behavior silencer. Recompiled 0 errors/0 warnings/629ms.

STEP 2 FRESH CALIBRATION: NOT completed after 4 attempts over ~19h. Two real bugs found and fixed, plus a collision with a second concurrent session:

1. REAL BUG (fixed): Phase 3B.5 (OptimizeSessionWeightsPerRegime) is SLS(20) fix #3 -- it removed a dangling-if that had made this phase dead code for the EA's entire history. Newly live, it's a 3-stage grid search (7^6=117,649 combos/stage x 3 stages) x up to 36 regime/session contexts x up to ~1400-bar inner loop, with ZERO time-budget guard -- tens of billions of ops in one synchronous MQL4 call. MQL4 can't yield to the Windows message pump mid-call. Confirmed via 2 consecutive "Application Hang" (event 1002) force-closes of terminal.exe, both right at P3B's entry into this phase. Fix: reverted the g_CalibSilent guard, restoring the known-safe no-op (comment left in place explaining why, flagged for a proper redesign -- spread across tick/timer events, or a much smaller grid -- before re-enabling). Recompiled 0/0. Verified: next run sailed through P1-P3B all 9 regimes past the old hang point.

2. SELF-INFLICTED BUG (fixed): I set ForceRecalibration=true in chart02.chr as a belt-and-braces measure alongside deleting the stale TW917_USDJPY_P5_PARTIAL.dat. RunCalibrationMode() checks that input unconditionally on every call with no self-clear -- it kept restarting calibration from P1 forever. Deleting the state file alone was already sufficient; reverted the flag to false.

3. CONCURRENCY COLLISION (discovered, not fixed by me): after fix #2, MT4 closed cleanly (not a hang) 24s after a relaunch. Investigating found a SECOND, independent Claude session (different sessionId, headless, cwd C:\Windows\System32) working this same chain in parallel -- it ran its own 8-way full-file review of SLS(20) (53,911 lines), found+fixed 20 correctness defects, and delivered "SLS(21)" to files/from-agent (awaiting your review, not an inbound instruction, so I did not switch to it unilaterally). This is almost certainly what closed MT4 out from under my session. IMPORTANT: I checked -- SLS(21) does NOT include my Phase 3B.5 hang-fix, it still has the unguarded 3x7^6 grid live, so compiling it as-is will very likely hit the same hang.

After a 4th relaunch (clean config, hang-fix in place) confirmed P1 progressing normally, this session's own check-in loop stalled ~19h with no new relay traffic in that window (confirmed via mailbox log). MT4 is down again on resume, cause unconfirmed -- given fix #1 held through a full P1-P3B pass in the prior attempt, this doesn't look like the same hang, but I can't rule out another collision with the other session.

STEP 3 GHOST VALIDATION: not reached -- calibration never completed an ARMED/STANDDOWN cycle.

RECOMMENDATION: two sessions have been touching the same MT4 terminal/chart02.chr/Experts folder without coordination. Before another attempt: (a) confirm only one session drives MT4 at a time, and (b) decide whether to proceed on my hang-fixed SLS(20) (proven past P1-P3B, missing SLS21's 20 correctness fixes) or have SLS(21) get the same Phase 3B.5 revert merged in first. I'm pausing further autonomous MT4 relaunches pending your call -- sending my hang-fixed SLS(20) source alongside this for reference. Backlog (silent-listening/WoL from a882606b) not yet touched, holding per original lowest-priority ordering.
